title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
snippets: NK (KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+ and FCGR3A+) cells
ILC1/NK have overlapping features of ILC1 and NKs, as described. Plasticity within ILC1 and ILC3 is also recognized
Fetal NK cells correlate with adult NK cells, but express higher levels of GZMM and GZMK, suggesting that they may be functionally competent
We identified 4 clusters of innate lymphocytes that were CD161(KLRB1)+ CD3(CD3D/CD3G)-, consisting of ILC1/3, ILC2, ILC1/Natural Killer (NK) and NK (KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+ and FCGR3A+) cells

title: Single-Cell Profiling Reveals Global Immune Responses During the Progression of Murine Epidermal Neoplasms
snippets: Natural killer cells play an important role in host immunity against pathogens and cancers. We identified one NK cell cluster based on its expression of Klr genes which encode molecules expressed on NK cells
we found activated NK cells in skin tumors indicated by higher levels of NK activation genes including Gzmb, Gzma, Eomes, Itga2 (Cd49b), and Klra8

title: Differential Co-Expression Network Analysis Reveals Key Hub-High Traffic Genes as Potential Therapeutic Targets for COVID-19 Pandemic
snippets: some important hub-high traffic genes involved in the cytotoxic activity of natural killer cells during COVID-19 were NCAM1, GZMA, NKG7, KLRD1, GZMB, and PRF1. Natural killer (NK) cells are key innate immune cells with a central role in killing virus-infected cells
